traces.mapping_rationale: Direct bank match for normalizing the raw `dd.mm.yyyy` date string before time-aware preprocessing.
Selected as the conservative outlier-trimming step for extreme item-price and item-count spikes before monthly aggregation.

traces.section: Aggregate Daily Sales To Monthly Target
traces.mapping_rationale: Direct bank match for summing `item_cnt_day` by `date_block_num`, `shop_id`, and `item_id` to define the monthly target table.
Selected to recover `item_category_id` from `items.csv` before category-aware feature branches.
Selected as the standard competition target clipping step for monthly demand values in `[0, 20]`.
Direct bank match for the standard `1,2,3,6,12` month target lag family at the shop-item level.

traces.section: Fill Missing Target Lags With Zero
traces.mapping_rationale: Selected to fill missing direct target-lag values with zero as the baseline no-prior-demand value.
Selected to drop the first 12 warmup months after introducing 12-step lag features.
Selected to join `item_categories.csv` before deriving compact category text features.
Selected to join `shops.csv` so shop-name text can be reduced into city-level categorical signals.
Selected as the text-derived feature block that maps raw shop and category names into `city`, `type`, and `subtype`.
Selected to encode the compact derived categorical features for tree-style reference models.

traces.section: Create First-Sale And Last-Sale Recency Features
traces.mapping_rationale: Selected to add first-sale and last-sale recency signals on top of the monthly forecasting table.

omitted_notebook_steps.note: Strong notebooks frequently materialize the full month grid with `product(...)`, but the current benchmark treats that as a documented workflow assumption rather than a standalone scored bank row.

The notebooks often build many additional lagged group means, such as month-item, month-shop, and month-category averages, but the current conservative bank does not score those as separate good rows.
Price-trend and revenue-trend branches were intentionally excluded from the current bank to keep the official-only benchmark spine narrower and easier to score.
